title: Cross-Session Roadmap Control Plane — Tool Specifications

tools.name: roadmap_init
tools.description: Injected at every session start, typically via read_messages or a new system-init hook. Returns the active roadmap DAG pruned to items whose dependencies are met or already in-progress. Past session cross-references are embedded in each item so the orchestrator can pick up mid-stream without rediscovery.

behavior.trigger: Automatic on session start. Orchestrator does NOT call this — the runtime calls it before the first user message.

items.title: task_board fleet visibility
items.context: Phase 2 session ses_189 identified that task_board only shows knowledge graph entries, not actual background tasks. Fix: coordination store must register task metadata at spawn time.
items.next_step: Extend task() tool to accept and register lane_id + role + files_modified metadata at spawn time. Wire into coordination store queries.

behavior.caching: The roadmap artifact sits at docs/json/roadmaps/active.v1.json, updated by roadmap_progress. roadmap_init reads this file and enriches it with the latest session findings.
behavior.file_dependency: docs/json/roadmaps/active.v1.json — the canonical live roadmap, NOT docs/json/roadmaps/opencode-desktop-phase3-roadmap.v1.json (which is the blueprint).

edge_cases: No roadmap artifact exists: return empty roadmap, suggest creating one via propose_roadmap
Orphaned items (depends_on item was deleted): warn, skip from next_actionable
All items completed: return completion summary, set status=complete

tools.name: roadmap_progress
tools.description: Called at session end (or on milestone within a session) to persist progress. Updates the active roadmap artifact with a new session entry and recalculates completion percentages up the phase tree.

behavior.caller: Orchestrator, typically during curate_context or generate_report
behavior.signature: roadmap_progress(item_id: string, status: 'not_started' | 'in_progress' | 'complete' | 'blocked' | 'deprecated', completion_pct: number, note: string, blocker?: { description: string, depends_on: string[] })
behavior.append_logic: Each update appends a line to docs/json/roadmaps/active.v1.jsonl (audit trail). The active.v1.json file is re-materialized from the latest state of each item.
behavior.recalculation: When an item reaches 100%, check all items that depend on it. If their deps are now all met, move them from 'blocked' to 'ready' and include them in next_actionable_items.

edge_cases: Item already at 100%: warn, allow override with force=true
completion_pct decreases: warn but allow (re-prioritization or descope)
status=blocked without blocker: require blocker field, reject otherwise

tools.name: roadmap_next
tools.description: Read-only query. Returns the top-N actionable items sorted by priority whose dependencies are all met, and whose completion_pct < 100. Designed to answer 'what should I work on next?' without reading the full roadmap.

behavior.caller: Orchestrator at session start or after completing a work item
behavior.signature: roadmap_next(limit: number = 5, phase_id?: string, include_deprecated: boolean = false)

items.title: task_board fleet visibility
items.next_step: Extend task() tool to register lane_id + role metadata at spawn time
items.context_summary: Phase 2 showed task_board is blind to background tasks. Fix before attempting any multi-lane session.

items.title: Set up SolidJS testing harness
items.context_summary: Phase 2 added 54 IPC tests but zero dialog component tests.

behavior.sorting: Priority (P1→P4), then effort (small→large), then dependency count (fewer deps first), then alphabetical.
behavior.filtering: Items with status=complete or status=deprecated are excluded unless include_deprecated=true. Items whose dependencies include an incomplete item are excluded unless show_blocked=true.

edge_cases: No actionable items: return empty list with status='all_complete' or 'all_blocked' with list of blockers
limit > total actionable: return all, no error
phase_id given but no items in phase: return empty with phase_summary

tools.name: roadmap_deprecate
tools.description: Removes a roadmap item from the active set without deleting its history. Sets status=deprecated with a TTL. Deprecated items are hidden from roadmap_next but visible in roadmap_progress history and roadmap_init --show-deprecated.

behavior.caller: Orchestrator or lane-owner secretary when a session discovers the item is no longer relevant
behavior.signature: roadmap_deprecate(item_id: string, reason: string, ttl_days: number = 30, replacement_item_id?: string)

output.reason: Lessons register was superseded by the findings registry — discover_findings covers the same need with richer metadata.

behavior.file_affected: docs/json/roadmaps/active.v1.json — item moved to deprecated[] array instead of items[]
behavior.revival: If a future session calls roadmap_progress on a deprecated item with status=in_progress, it moves back to active automatically with a warning. This handles the case where the item was deprecated prematurely.
behavior.dependency_cascade: When an item is deprecated, all items that depend exclusively on it are automatically blocked with reason='Upstream dependency deprecated'. If they also depend on other items, they remain ready if those deps are met.

edge_cases: Item already deprecated: extend TTL, keep existing reason unless new reason provided
Item has dependent items: list them in output, cascade block
replacement_item_id doesn't exist: warn but allow (may be future item)

tools.name: roadmap_prioritize
tools.description: Changes an item's priority within its phase, or moves an item to a different phase. Roadmap order is recalculated on next roadmap_next call.

behavior.signature: roadmap_prioritize(item_id: string, priority: 'P1' | 'P2' | 'P3' | 'P4', phase_id?: string, order_hint?: number)
behavior.recalculation: After re-prioritization, roadmap_next is re-evaluated and the new order is returned in output so the orchestrator sees the impact immediately.

edge_cases: Item doesn't exist: reject with available items list
phase_id changes but phase doesn't exist: create phase automatically with status=ready, warn
Conflicting order_hint: items at same order_hint sort alphabetically by item_id

file_structure.blueprint: docs/json/roadmaps/opencode-desktop-phase3-roadmap.v1.json — the master plan, updated infrequently (phase boundaries)
file_structure.active: docs/json/roadmaps/active.v1.json — the live snapshot, updated by every roadmap_progress call
file_structure.audit_trail: docs/json/roadmaps/progress.v1.jsonl — append-only log of all roadmap_progress, roadmap_deprecate, roadmap_prioritize calls

session_integration.startup: roadmap_init runs automatically → injects next_actionable_items into orchestrator context
session_integration.during: Orchestrator can call roadmap_next at any decision point: 'what should I work on next?'
session_integration.end: Orchestrator calls roadmap_progress for each completed/modified item during generate_report
session_integration.secretary_integration: Lane-owner secretary calls roadmap_progress when a lane completes, and roadmap_next to pick its next lane
session_integration.cross_session_findings_integration: discover_findings matches against roadmap item IDs. A finding tagged with item_id=TEL-001 shows up when that item is queried via roadmap_next.

pre_existing_findings_from_phase_2: Each session has ~5 edge case categories per tool, derived from actual session failures
roadmap_progress edge cases came from the 'critic rejected prefab' timing issue — without progress tracking, we didn't know lanes were in wrong state
roadmap_deprecate came from FI-002 (onSave already fixed) — the follow-up issue was stale but had no deprecation mechanism
roadmap_next came from session start blind spots — every session rediscovered the context from scratch
